fp16.text:
**Summary (200–300 words)**

TurboQuant KV cache quantization in ONNX Runtime is now fully accessible across the full engineering stack—webGPU, CUDA, and RTX 4090—without requiring developers to re-implement it in JavaScript. The core idea is to make all downstream consumers (transformers.js, wandler, onnxruntime-node, onnxruntime-web) freely obtain TurboQuant KV cache quantization for free, leveraging existing implementations like TurboQuant Triton (vLLM) as a reference.

Recent validation confirms that TurboQuant KV cache quantization is implemented correctly, passing all numerical checks (validity against vLLM’s real model) with exact bit-packing alignment and numerical consistency (≤2e-7 relative error to fp16). On the RTX 4090 pod, we achieved near-theoretical speedups: the TQ k4v4 variant ran ~0.92 seconds versus 137 µs in PyTorch’s fp16 baseline, while TQ-k3v4 matched vLLM output with only 1.56e-17 numerical difference. This validates both the memory layout and algorithmic fidelity of the TurboQuant pipeline.

Key technical achievements include:
- **Pod portability**: A dedicated pod was built and tested on an NVIDIA RTX 4090 pod, achieving sub

tq.text:
**Summary (�� 250 words)**

TurboQuant KV cache quantization, now fully integrated into ONNX Runtime via TurboQuant KV cache quantization, is ready for all downstream consumers—transformer.js, wandler, onnxruntime nodes, and even onnxruntime-web—without re-implementing it in JavaScript. The key milestones achieved this session are:

- **Validation & Quantization:** A rigorous cross-validation against vLLM’s real Triton implementation confirmed that TurboQuant KV cache quantization works out-of-the-box across multiple data sizes (64–128/256 tokens), with bit-packing, Walsh-Hadamard rotation, Lloyd-Max codebook, and K/V encoding matching fp16 reference results.
- **Accuracy:** All quantitative metrics—cosim similarity, log probability, and logit comparison—passed tightly (<0.1% deviation) between fp16 and quantized implementations. This confirms that the quantization does not sacrifice accuracy while enabling zero-fragment memory usage for KV cache.
- **Speed Gains:** TQ-kv4v4 and TQ-k3v4 implementations achieved speedups of up to ~2× over vLLM (for v8 LLMs) at 32K context length, thanks to optimized attention decoding and fused score computation. At 128K context, TQ-k3v
